---
applyTo: '**/*.4dm'
---

# 4D Test Instructions

## Running Tests

```bash
# Dataless (no data file — for logic-only tests):
<tool4dbin> --project <projectfile> --dataless --skip-onstartup --startup-method <testmethod>

# With data (to query actual database content):
<tool4dbin> --project <projectfile> --skip-onstartup --startup-method <testmethod>
```

- Omit `--dataless` when the test needs to read or write database records.
- Always end the test method with `QUIT 4D(0)` so tool4d exits cleanly.

## Console Output

Use `LOG EVENT` to print to stdout (the only way to get output from tool4d):

```4d
LOG EVENT(Into system standard outputs; "message")
```

## tool4d Location

Check in order:
1. `tool4d.app/Contents/MacOS/tool4d` in project
2. `tool4d` in $PATH
3. `$TOOL4DBIN` environment variable
4. `$HOME/Library/Application Support/Code/User/globalStorage/4D.4d-analyzer/tool4d/` (use `find` to locate the latest version)
5. If not found, tool4d can be downloaded at https://resources-download.4d.com/release/<product-line>/<version>/<build>/<os>/tool4d_<os or arch>.tar.xz
- product-line: tool4d product line (default defined [here](https://github.com/4d/tool4d-action/blob/main/versions.json), ex: 20.x)
- version: tool4d version (default defined [here](https://github.com/4d/tool4d-action/blob/main/versions.json), ex: 20.3)
- build-method: tool4d build number (default latest)
6. ask the user to install `tool4d`, available in https://product-download.4d.com/release/21%20Rx/21%20R2/100196/mac/tool4d_arm64.tar.xz

Important: tool4d currently cannot resolve project dependencies. If the opened project has dependencies, an error may be returned on opening, or may occur when the code tries to run a dependency asset.
